name: Deep Fried Shrimp Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely mince shrimp, bacon, onion or water chestnuts together. Place the mixture in a bowl. Remove the crusts from bread and dice the bread into very fine cubes. Add the cubes to the shrimp mixture. Stir in salt, pepper, egg, and 2 tablespoons of corn starch until combined.
2. Heat oil in a wok to the highest setting until a small piece of bread crumb frys to a golden brown color when tested. If the crumb fries to a dark brown too quickly, the oil is too hot. Turn off the heat, allow the oil to cool for 5 minutes before testing again.
3. Once the oil reaches the proper temperature, form shrimp mixture into 1-inch diameter balls using about 2 teaspoons of mixture for each ball. Drop the balls into the hot oil and fry for 3 minutes. Flip the balls to ensure they fry evenly until golden brown. Remove the balls from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Repeat with remaining mixture. Place shrimp balls on a serving platter and garnish with parsley or other raw vegetable.
